subject: [PATCH] mm/swap_state: remove unnecessary lru_add_drain() from readahead

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Reviewer Shakeel Butt noted that while the patch is a good cleanup effort, he suggested adding visibility into lru_add_drain_all() callers through tracepoints to better understand their behavior.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>

Thanks for pushing this. JP was also looking into LRU lock contention sources.
Particularly we lack visibiluty into the lru_add_drain_all() callers. The idea
was to add tracepoints to tracks such callers. (Just nudging you towards it :P)

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch removes unnecessary calls to lru_add_drain() in the readahead functions swap_cluster_readahead() and swap_vma_readahead(). The drain was a leftover from an older version of the code and serves no functional purpose for the callers. The folios added to the swap cache by the readahead loop are naturally drained as they fill up the per-CPU LRU batch, eliminating the need for unconditional draining. This patch addresses performance issues on high-CPU systems running memory-pressured workloads.

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Barry Song pointed out that the patch does not address a specific case where do_swap_page() and wp_can_reuse_anon_folio() call lru_add_drain(), which is necessary for accurate reference counting, and requested that these calls be preserved.

Reviewer Barry Song asked if there is a measurable workload improvement after removing the unnecessary lru_add_drain() calls and requested that performance data be included in the patch
reviews.raw_body:
right. as i can see the below in do_swap_page():

        /*
         * If we want to map a page that's in the swapcache writable, we
         * have to detect via the refcount if we're really the exclusive
         * owner. Try removing the extra reference from the local LRU
         * caches if required.
         */
        if ((vmf->flags & FAULT_FLAG_WRITE) &&
            !folio_test_ksm(folio) && !folio_test_lru(folio))
                lru_add_drain();

and the below in wp_can_reuse_anon_folio():

        if (!folio_test_lru(folio))
                /*
                 * We cannot easily detect+handle references from
                 * remote LRU caches or references to LRU folios.
                 */
                lru_add_drain();

---

Do we see a workload improvement? If yes, can we put the data?

Thanks
Barry

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that lru lock contention is a source of issue in production, specifically on a workload with heavy swap activity, and plans to verify the patch's impact by waiting for kernel upgrade.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Barry!

So lru lock contention is a source of issue in the meta fleet.

This problem was specifically seen when I ran `perf lock contention -a -b`
in production on a workload that has a really big anon heap and heavy swap
activity.

When I tried to trace with bpftrace who was the biggest consumer, it was
readahead.

It is easy to run perf and bpftrace on prod on this specific workload, but
more difficult to flash a new kernel and see results. The easiest would be
when kernel upgrade happens and this patch lands to see the difference and
I can report back.

Thanks,
Usama

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer expressed concern that removing lru_add_drain() might overlook potential corner cases, and suggested queuing the patch for testing once the mm tree is ready.
reviews.raw_body:
we might be missing any corner cases.

I would be happy to see it queued for testing once the mm tree is
ready to accept new patches. Is it too late at the moment?

Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Barry Song <baohua@kernel.org>

Thanks,
Barry
